blurb:
Panda came through a court case with her eight puppies, and based on her scars, she's been through more than any dog should have to. That's all behind her now. She just finished treatment for heartworm disease and is officially ready to start over at seven years old. Don't tell her she's seven though because this girl still loves to get out and romp with dogs half her age.

She's not a fan of small dogs but gets along great with bigger ones who can keep up with her energy. She loves all people and would be perfectly happy with a comfy sofa for afternoon naps between play sessions. After everything she's been through, Panda deserves someone who's going to spoil her rotten.

To adopt Panda, email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org. She's in a foster home now, so you'll need to go through the rescue to meet her.

His name is Goober and he's a chocolate lab, which honestly tells you most of what you need to know. He's probably friendly, food-motivated, and thinks every person he meets is his new best friend. Labs are like that. His bio is pretty sparse too, so we're going with what we can see in his photo and what we know about chocolate labs in general.

He looks like the kind of dog who greets you at the door like you've been gone for years when you just went to get the mail. Labs are notorious for being happy, loyal, and slightly obsessed with tennis balls, treats, and swimming. If you have a pool, lake access, or even just a kiddie pool in the backyard, he's probably going to lose his mind with excitement.

Chocolate labs tend to be the goofballs of the lab family, and with a name like Goober, he's probably living up to that reputation. He's likely great with kids, other dogs, and anyone willing to pet him or throw something for him to fetch. These dogs are made for families who want a steady, loving companion who's always up for whatever you're doing.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta and you can contact them through their Petfinder listing to learn more about Goober and get the adoption process started.

Our Pals Place
Marietta, GA
Contact through Petfinder
scoring_notes:
• Perfect family dog appeals to broadest reader demographic
• Chocolate lab popularity ensures high adoption interest
• Endearing name and personality create immediate emotional connection

blurb:
Someone named this cat Pork Tenderloin and honestly, that's the kind of energy we need more of in the world. He's an adult male with short hair and what appears to be a very serious expression in his photo, like he's contemplating whether his name is actually helping his adoption chances.

This listing doesn't have much of a bio, so we're going off his photo and that incredible name. He looks like the kind of cat who would sit in your kitchen while you cook dinner, judging your seasoning choices but staying close enough to supervise. Definitely has opinions about things. Probably the type to claim one specific chair in your house and make it clear that's his spot now.

Good Mews doesn't post detailed bios for all their cats, but they're a no-kill, cage-free shelter where you can visit and get to know the animals before deciding. Pork Tenderloin is waiting there to meet someone who appreciates both a good name and whatever personality comes with it. Submit an inquiry online or call to set up a visit.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org
Tues-Sat 10am-4pm, Sun 1pm-4pm, closed Mondays
scoring_notes:
• Unforgettable name creates instant reader connection and shareability
• Relatable personality description appeals to cat lovers seeking character
• Unique story angle drives newsletter engagement and social media buzz

blurb:
When Morrie arrived in late fall, he was basically skin and bones with a terrible skin infection. Months of care in his foster home turned him into the 18-pound spunky Italian Greyhound he was meant to be. He's estimated to be nine or ten years old, but after years of neglect, it's hard to tell his real age. What's not hard to tell is that this boy has personality for days.

Morrie spends his time making his foster mom laugh because he's genuinely goofy and hilarious. He gets along great with all dogs and clearly has no idea he's a senior. He's looking for his very own person to show him what life is like as a beloved family member instead of a neglected afterthought.

If you want to meet Morrie, email adrienne@rescuemega.org or fill out an application at www.rmgdogs.org. After what he's been through, he's earned the right to be someone's favorite.
scoring_notes:
• Incredible transformation from neglect to thriving creates hope
• Senior dog with puppy personality appeals to mature adopters
• Months of recovery time shows urgent need for permanent home

blurb:
Hazel Jordan is a Nova Scotia Duck Tolling Retriever, which is basically a dog breed most people have never heard of but should probably know about. She doesn't have a proper bio either, so we're going off her photo and breed knowledge. Tollers are the redheads of the retriever world, and they're known for being energetic, smart, and having a slightly mischievous streak.

She's got that beautiful red coat and probably the personality to match. These dogs were bred to lure ducks by playing on the shoreline, so she's likely playful, athletic, and probably thinks fetch is the greatest invention ever. If you throw something, she's bringing it back. If you don't throw something, she's probably going to find something anyway and drop it at your feet with a look that says "your move."

She's going to need someone who appreciates an active, intelligent dog. Tollers do great with families and usually love water if you happen to live near a lake or creek. She's probably going to want to be involved in whatever you're doing, whether that's yard work, hiking, or just sitting on the couch.

Our Pals Place handles adoptions through their Petfinder page. Contact them directly to learn more about Hazel Jordan and start the adoption process.

Our Pals Place
Marietta, GA
Contact through Petfinder
scoring_notes:
• Rare breed creates educational opportunity and reader curiosity
• Active lifestyle appeal targets engaged pet owner demographic
• Unique breed story drives newsletter readership and sharing

blurb:
Chocolate Orange has a name that sounds like a fancy dessert, and honestly, she's probably just as sweet. She's listed as young, so she's past the crazy kitten phase but still has that playful energy that makes cats fun to have around.

Without a detailed bio, we're going on her photo and her age. She looks like she's got some beautiful coloring that matches her name, and young cats are usually the perfect balance of playful and chill. They'll chase a feather toy around for twenty minutes, then settle down for a nap in whatever warm spot they can find.

Young adult cats are often the easiest to adopt. They're past the destructive kitten phase but still have years of companionship ahead of them. They've usually figured out litter boxes and basic cat etiquette, but they're not set in their ways yet.

DeKalb County Animal Services has her available for adoption in Chamblee. You can visit to see what her actual personality is like and whether she lives up to her sweet-sounding name.

DeKalb County Animal Services
Chamblee, GA
Contact through Petfinder listing
scoring_notes:
• Perfect young adult age appeals to broad adopter base
• Sweet dessert name creates memorable reader connection
• County shelter pet needs newsletter exposure for visibility

blurb:
Patriot is a senior male cat who's probably seen some things in his time. The shelter doesn't have a detailed bio for him, but his photo shows a handsome orange tabby with white markings who looks like he knows how to settle into a comfortable routine. Senior cats often make the best companions once you get past the idea that older means less fun.

He's at that age where he's done with drama and just wants a calm place to spend his days. Senior cats usually know what they like and don't waste time with nonsense. They're also grateful for a second chance in a way that's different from younger animals. Patriot probably won't be climbing your curtains or knocking things off your counters at 3am.

If you've been thinking about adding a cat to your household, seniors like Patriot are often overlooked but make incredible pets. They're past the destructive kitten phase and ready to just be good company. Visit Good Mews to meet him and see if you click.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org
Tues-Sat 10am-4pm, Sun 1pm-4pm, closed Mondays
scoring_notes:
• Senior cats need urgent advocacy as they're often overlooked
• Strong appeal to readers seeking low-maintenance companions
• Educational angle helps shift perceptions about older pet adoption

blurb:
Beignet is an adult female cat with a name that makes you think of New Orleans and powdered sugar, which feels like a good start. Her listing doesn't include a personality description, but her photo shows a pretty tabby with distinctive markings who looks comfortable and alert. Sometimes the cats without detailed bios are the ones who surprise you most when you meet them in person.

She's at Good Mews, which is a cage-free environment, so she's been socializing with other cats and getting used to people coming and going. That usually means she's adaptable and not easily stressed by changes. Adult female cats like Beignet tend to be independent but affectionate when they want to be.

Without a proper bio to go on, meeting Beignet in person is really the only way to know if she's the right fit. Good Mews lets you spend time with their cats before deciding, which is exactly what you'd want to do with someone like her who's a bit of a mystery right now.

Good Mews Animal Foundation
3805 Robinson Road, Marietta, GA 30067
(770) 499-2287 | adopt@goodmews.org

scoring_notes:

blurb:
Luke is a young German Shepherd and Belgian Malinois mix who doesn't have much of a bio on file yet, so we're making one up based on his photo. Look at that face and tell us he doesn't have opinions about everything. He's probably the kind of dog who watches you make coffee in the morning and has thoughts about your technique.

He's got that shepherd intensity written all over him, which means he's going to be smart, loyal, and probably need a job to do. Even if that job is just following you around the house making sure you're doing everything correctly. These breeds are known for being devoted to their people and having energy to burn.

Since he's young and clearly a working breed mix, he's going to need someone who can keep up with him mentally and physically. A fenced yard would be perfect, and if you're the kind of person who likes long hikes or runs, even better. He's the type who will probably excel at whatever training you throw at him once he settles in.

Our Pals Place is in Marietta and handles adoptions through Petfinder. Check their page for contact information and how to get the adoption process started.

Our Pals Place
Marietta, GA
Contact through Petfinder
scoring_notes:
• High-energy working breed appeals to active readers
• Intelligent breed mix attracts experienced dog owners
• Young age creates urgency for proper training and socialization

blurb:
Someone tied Bubba and his brother Simba to a post on the playground at Austin Elementary in Dunwoody and walked away. Hours passed. The dogs had nice collars and harnesses and were so calm and loving with everyone who approached them, but no one came back. A week later, it was clear no one was coming. Now they need a home where they'll never be abandoned again.

Bubba and Simba are bonded seven-year-old yellow lab mixes who run outside by side and panic if they get separated for more than a few seconds. They're housebroken, crate trained, know basic commands, and love toys, other dogs, kids, walks, and car rides. They're fully vetted and ready to be spoiled rotten by someone who understands that two dogs are sometimes better than one.

They need to be adopted together, but if you've got the space and the heart for both of them, email adrienne@rescuemega.org or apply at www.rmgdogs.org. They've earned the right to never worry about being left behind again.
scoring_notes:
• Heartbreaking abandonment story will move readers to action
• Bonded pair needs special home creating newsletter urgency
• Well-trained dogs abandoned at school creates community connection

blurb:
Maple Leaf is a kitten with a name that suggests someone was feeling very autumn when she arrived. She's got that classic kitten energy that's going to keep you entertained and probably wake you up at 3am because she found a hair tie under the couch.

She's young enough that her personality is still developing, which means you get to be part of shaping who she becomes. Kittens this age are usually fearless, curious about everything, and ready to turn any household object into a toy. Your shoelaces will never be the same.

She'll need kitten shots, spaying when she's old enough, and probably a friend to play with. Kittens do better in pairs, so if you've been thinking about getting two cats, this might be perfect timing. Two kittens will tire each other out instead of using you as their personal jungle gym.

DeKalb County Animal Services is in Chamblee. You can visit to meet Maple Leaf and see if she's as energetic in person as we're imagining she is.

DeKalb County Animal Services
Chamblee, GA
Contact through Petfinder listing
scoring_notes:
• High kitten appeal with playful energy attracts families
• County shelter location suggests urgency for adoption
• Generic kitten story lacks unique hook for newsletter feature

blurb:
Brie is a gorgeous medium-haired cat who doesn't have much of a bio yet, so we're going to make one up based on her stunning photo. Look at those eyes. She's clearly the type of cat who sits in the perfect sunny spot by the window and makes you wonder what she's thinking about. Probably your snack schedule.

She's got that classic tortoiseshell coloring with beautiful long fur that's going to require some brushing, but honestly, most cats love being brushed once they get used to it. It becomes bonding time. She looks like she'd be content to sit with you while you work from home, maybe occasionally walking across your keyboard to remind you she exists.

Since her actual personality is a mystery, you'll get to discover who she really is. That's half the fun of adopting an adult cat. You might get a lap cat, you might get a playful hunter, or you might get both depending on her mood.

Three Paws Rescue is based in Chamblee. You can find Brie's full listing on Petfinder and reach out through their adoption process to learn more about her real personality.

Three Paws Rescue
Chamblee, GA
Contact through Petfinder listing
scoring_notes:
• Beautiful tortoiseshell with mystery personality creates intrigue
• Perfect work-from-home companion appeals to remote workers
• Lacks compelling backstory but stunning appearance compensates
